name: aiohttp-client-cache

metadata.name: aiohttp-client-cache
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# aiohttp-client-cache

[![Build status](https://github.com/requests-cache/aiohttp-client-cache/actions/workflows/build.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/requests-cache/aiohttp-client-cache/actions)
[![Documentation Status](https://img.shields.io/readthedocs/aiohttp-client-cache/latest?label=docs)](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)
[![Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/requests-cache/aiohttp-client-cache/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=I6PNLYTILM)](https://codecov.io/gh/requests-cache/aiohttp-client-cache)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/aiohttp-client-cache?color=blue)](https://pypi.org/project/aiohttp-client-cache)
[![Conda](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/aiohttp-client-cache?color=blue)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/aiohttp-client-cache)
[![PyPI - Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/aiohttp-client-cache)](https://pypi.org/project/aiohttp-client-cache)
[![PyPI - Format](https://img.shields.io/pypi/format/aiohttp-client-cache?color=blue)](https://pypi.org/project/aiohttp-client-cache)

**aiohttp-client-cache** is an async persistent cache for [aiohttp](https://docs.aiohttp.org)
client requests, based on [requests-cache](https://github.com/reclosedev/requests-cache).

# Features

- **Ease of use:** Use as a [drop-in replacement](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user_guide.html)
  for `aiohttp.ClientSession`
- **Customization:** Works out of the box with little to no config, but with plenty of options
  available for customizing cache
  [expiration](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user_guide.html#cache-expiration)
  and other [behavior](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user_guide.html#cache-options)
- **Persistence:** Includes several [storage backends](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/backends.html):
  SQLite, DynamoDB, MongoDB, DragonflyDB and Redis.

# Quickstart

First, install with pip (python 3.8+ required):

```bash
pip install aiohttp-client-cache[all]
```

**Note:**
Adding `[all]` will install optional dependencies for all supported backends. When adding this
library to your application, you can include only the dependencies you actually need; see individual
backend docs and [pyproject.toml](https://github.com/requests-cache/aiohttp-client-cache/blob/main/pyproject.toml)
for details.

## Basic Usage

Next, use [aiohttp_client_cache.CachedSession](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/modules/aiohttp_client_cache.session.html#aiohttp_client_cache.session.CachedSession)
in place of [aiohttp.ClientSession](https://docs.aiohttp.org/en/stable/client_reference.html#aiohttp.ClientSession).
To briefly demonstrate how to use it:

**Replace this:**

```python
from aiohttp import ClientSession

async with ClientSession() as session:
    await session.get('http://httpbin.org/delay/1')
```

**With this:**

```python
from aiohttp_client_cache import CachedSession, SQLiteBackend

async with CachedSession(cache=SQLiteBackend('demo_cache')) as session:
    await session.get('http://httpbin.org/delay/1')
```

The URL in this example adds a delay of 1 second, simulating a slow or rate-limited website.
With caching, the response will be fetched once, saved to `demo_cache.sqlite`, and subsequent
requests will return the cached response near-instantly.

## Configuration

Several options are available to customize caching behavior. This example demonstrates a few of them:

```python
# fmt: off
from aiohttp_client_cache import SQLiteBackend

cache = SQLiteBackend(
    cache_name='~/.cache/aiohttp-requests.db',  # For SQLite, this will be used as the filename
    expire_after=60*60,                         # By default, cached responses expire in an hour
    urls_expire_after={'*.fillmurray.com': -1}, # Requests for any subdomain on this site will never expire
    allowed_codes=(200, 418),                   # Cache responses with these status codes
    allowed_methods=['GET', 'POST'],            # Cache requests with these HTTP methods
    include_headers=True,                       # Cache requests with different headers separately
    ignored_params=['auth_token'],              # Keep using the cached response even if this param changes
    timeout=2.5,                                # Connection timeout for SQLite backend
)
```

# More Info

To learn more, see:

- [User Guide](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user_guide.html)
- [Cache Backends](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/backends.html)
- [API Reference](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference.html)
- [Examples](https://aiohttp-client-cache.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples.html)

# Feedback

If there is a feature you want, if you've discovered a bug, or if you have other general feedback, please
[create an issue](https://github.com/requests-cache/aiohttp-client-cache/issues/new/choose) for it!

modules.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session
documentation: Core functions for cache configuration

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CacheMixin

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CacheMixin._request
documentation: Wrapper around {py:meth}`.SessionClient._request` that adds caching

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CacheMixin._refresh_cached_response
documentation: Checks if the cached response is still valid using conditional requests if supported

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CacheMixin.close
documentation: Close both aiohttp connector and any backend connection(s) on contextmanager exit

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CacheMixin.disabled
documentation:
Temporarily disable the cache

Example:

    >>> async with CachedSession() as session:
    >>>     await session.get('http://httpbin.org/ip')
    >>>     async with session.disabled():
    >>>         # Will return a new response, not a cached one
    >>>         await session.get('http://httpbin.org/ip')

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CacheMixin.delete_expired_responses
documentation: Remove all expired responses from the cache
A mixin class for {py:class}`aiohttp.ClientSession` that adds caching support

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.session.CachedSession
documentation:
A drop-in replacement for {py:class}`aiohttp.ClientSession` that adds caching support

Args:
: cache: A cache backend object. See {py:mod}`aiohttp_client_cache.backends` for

  : options. If not provided, an in-memory cache will be used.

modules.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures
documentation:
Utilities for dynamically modifying function signatures.

The purpose of this is to generate thorough documentation of arguments without a ton of
copy-pasted text. This applies to both Sphinx docs hosted on readthedocs, as well as autocompletion,
type checking, etc. within an IDE or other editor.

Currently this is used to add the following backend-specific connection details:

* Function signatures
* Type annotations
* Argument docs

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures.extend_signature
documentation:
A function decorator that modifies the target function's signature and docstring with:

- Params from superclass
- Params from target function
- (optional) Params from additional functions called by the target function

This also makes `forge` optional. If it's not installed, or if there's a problem with
modifying a signature, this will just log the error and return the function with its original
signature.

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures.get_combined_revision
documentation: Combine the parameters of all revisions into a single revision

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures.deduplicate_kwargs
documentation:
If a list of params contains one or more variadic keyword args (e.g., `**kwargs`),
ensure there are no duplicates and move it to the end.

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures.copy_docstrings
documentation:
Copy 'Args' documentation from one or more template functions to a target function.
Assumes Google-style docstrings.

Args:

: target_function: Function to modify
  template_functions: Functions containing docstrings to apply to `target_function`

Returns:

: Target function with modified docstring

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures._split_docstring
documentation:
Split a docstring into the following sections, if present:

* Function summary
* Argument descriptions
* Return value description

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.signatures._combine_args_sections
documentation: Combine 'Args' sections from multiple functions into one section, removing any duplicates

documentation: Check for a matching per-URL expiration, if any

documentation: Determine if headers contain cache directives **that we currently support**

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.compose_refresh_headers
documentation: Returns headers containing directives for conditional requests if the cached headers support it

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.parse_http_date
documentation: Attempt to parse an HTTP (RFC 5322-compatible) timestamp

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.parse_http_date
documentation: Attempt to parse an HTTP (RFC 5322-compatible) timestamp

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.split_kv_directive
documentation:
Split a cache directive into a `(header_value, int)` key-value pair, if possible;
otherwise just `(header_value, True)`.

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.convert_to_utc_naive
documentation:
All internal datetimes are UTC and timezone-naive. Convert any user/header-provided
datetimes to the same format.

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.utcnow
documentation: Get the current time in UTC, as a timezone-naive datetime
TODO: This could be replaced with timezone-aware datetimes, but this will cause problems with
existing cache data. It would be best to do this at the same time as a release that includes
changes to request matching logic (i.e., new cache keys).

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.url_match
documentation:
Determine if a URL matches a pattern

Args:
    url: URL to test. Its base URL (without protocol) will be used.
    pattern: Glob pattern to match against. A recursive wildcard will be added if not present

Example:
    >>> url_match('https://httpbin.org/delay/1', 'httpbin.org/delay')
    True
    >>> url_match('https://httpbin.org/stream/1', 'httpbin.org/*/1')
    True
    >>> url_match('https://httpbin.org/stream/2', 'httpbin.org/*/1')
    False

documentation: Initialize from request info and CacheBackend settings

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.CacheActions.from_settings

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.CacheActions.expires

class_variables.name: aiohttp_client_cache.cache_control.CacheActions.revalidate
documentation: Note: Revalidation is not currently implemented

A dataclass that contains info on specific actions to take for a given cache item.
This is determined by a combination of CacheBackend settings and request + response headers.
If multiple sources are provided, they will be used in the following order of precedence:

1. Cache-Control request headers (if enabled)
2. Cache-Control response headers (if enabled)
3. Per-request expiration
4. Per-URL expiration
5. Per-session expiration

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.filesystem.FileBackend
documentation:
Backend that stores cached responses as files on the local filesystem.

Notes:
: - Requires [aiofiles](https://github.com/Tinche/aiofiles) and [aiosqlite](https://aiosqlite.omnilib.dev).
  - Response paths will be in the format `<cache_name>/responses/<cache_key>`.
  - Redirects are stored in a SQLite database, located at `<cache_name>/redirects.sqlite`.

Args:

: cache_name: Base directory for cache files
  use_temp: Store cache files in a temp directory (e.g., `/tmp/http_cache/`).

  > Note: if `cache_name` is an absolute path, this option will be ignored.

  autoclose: Close any active backend connections when the session is closed
  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:class}`.CacheBackend`

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.filesystem.FileCache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.filesystem.FileCache._try_io
documentation: Attempt an I/O operation, and either ignore errors or re-raise them as KeyErrors

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.filesystem.FileCache.clear
documentation: Note: Currently this is a blocking operation

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.filesystem.FileCache.paths
documentation: Get file paths to all cached responses
A dictionary-like interface to files on the local filesystem

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.redis.RedisBackend
documentation:
Async cache backend for [Redis](https://redis.io)

Notes:
: - Requires [redis-py](https://redis-py.readthedocs.io)
  - Accepts keyword arguments for {py:class}`redis.asyncio.client.Redis`

Args:

: cache_name: Used as a namespace (prefix for hash key)
  address: Redis server URI
  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:class}`.CacheBackend` or backend connection

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.redis.RedisCache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.redis.RedisCache.get_connection
documentation: Lazy-initialize redis connection

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.redis.RedisCache.bulk_delete
documentation: Requires redis version >=2.4

An async interface for caching objects in Redis.

Args:

: namespace: namespace to use
  collection_name: name of the hash map stored in redis
  connection: An existing connection object to reuse instead of creating a new one
  address: Address of Redis server
  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:class}`aioredis.Redis`

Note: The hash key name on the redis server will be `namespace:collection_name`.

variables.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.dynamodb.MAX_ITEM_SIZE
documentation: 400KB

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.dynamodb.DynamoDBBackend
documentation:
Async cache backend for [DynamoDB](https://aws.amazon.com/dynamodb)

Notes:
: - Requires [aioboto3](https://aioboto3.readthedocs.io)
  - Accepts keyword arguments for {py:meth}`boto3.session.Session.client`
  - See [DynamoDB Service Resource](https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/services/dynamodb.html#service-resource)
    for more usage details.
  - DynamoDB has a maximum item size of 400KB. If an item exceeds this size, it will not be
    written to the cache.

Args:

: cache_name: Table name to use
  key_attr_name: The name of the field to use for keys in the DynamoDB document
  val_attr_name: The name of the field to use for values in the DynamoDB document
  create_if_not_exists: Whether or not to attempt to create the DynamoDB table
  context: An existing [ResourceCreatorContext](https://aioboto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html)

  > to reuse instead of creating a new one

  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:class}`.CacheBackend` or backend connection

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.dynamodb.DynamoDbCache
documentation:
An async interface for caching objects in a DynamoDB key-store

The actual key name on the dynamodb server will be `namespace:key`.
In order to deal with how dynamodb stores data/keys, all values must be serialized.

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.mongodb.MongoDBBackend
documentation:
Async cache backend for [MongoDB](https://www.mongodb.com)

Notes:
: - Requires [motor](https://motor.readthedocs.io)
  - Accepts keyword arguments for {py:class}`pymongo.MongoClient`

Args:

: cache_name: Database name
  connection: Optional client object to use instead of creating a new one
  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:class}`.CacheBackend` or backend connection

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.mongodb.MongoDBCache
documentation:
An async interface for caching objects in MongoDB

Args:

: db_name: database name (be careful with production databases)
  collection_name: collection name
  connection: MongoDB connection instance to use instead of creating a new one
  kwargs: Additional keyword args for {py:class}`~motor.motor_asyncio.AsyncIOMotorClient`

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.mongodb.MongoDBPickleCache
documentation: Same as {py:class}`MongoDBCache`, but pickles values before saving

documentation: Reset the stream reader to re-read a streamed response

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.response.CachedResponse._released
documentation: No-op/placeholder properties and methods that don't apply to a CachedResponse, but provide
compatibility with aiohttp.ClientResponse

A dataclass containing cached response information, used for serialization.
It will mostly behave the same as a {py:class}`aiohttp.ClientResponse` that has been read,
with some additional cache-related info.

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.response.CachedStreamReader
documentation:
A StreamReader loaded from previously consumed response content. This feeds cached data into
the stream so it can support all the same behavior as the original stream: async iteration,
chunked reads, etc.

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.sqlite_template
documentation: Template function to get an accurate function signature for {py:func}`sqlite3.connect`

functions.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite._get_cache_filename
documentation: Get resolved path for database file

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLiteBackend
documentation:
Async cache backend for [SQLite](https://www.sqlite.org)

Notes:
: - Requires [aiosqlite](https://aiosqlite.omnilib.dev)
  - Accepts keyword arguments for {py:func}`sqlite3.connect` / {py:func}`aiosqlite.connect`
  - The path to the database file will be `<cache_name>` (or `<cache_name>.sqlite` if no
    file extension is specified)

Args:

: cache_name: Database filename
  use_temp: Store database in a temp directory (e.g., `/tmp/http_cache.sqlite`).

  > Note: if `cache_name` is an absolute path, this option will be ignored.

  fast_save: Increase cache write performance, but with the possibility of data loss. See

  : [pragma: synchronous](http://www.sqlite.org/pragma.html#pragma_synchronous) for
    details.

  autoclose: Close any active backend connections when the session is closed
  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:class}`.CacheBackend` or backend connection

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLiteCache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLiteCache._init_db
documentation: Initialize the database, if it hasn't already been

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLiteCache.__del__
documentation:
If the aiosqlite connection is still open when this object is deleted, force its thread
to close by stopping its internal queue. This is basically a last resort to avoid hanging
the application if this backend is used without the CachedSession contextmanager.

Note: Since this uses internal attributes, it has the potential to break in future versions
of aiosqlite.

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLiteCache.bulk_commit
documentation:
Contextmanager to more efficiently write a large number of records at once

Example:

    >>> cache = SQLiteCache('test')
    >>> async with cache.bulk_commit():
    ...     for i in range(1000):
    ...         await cache.write(f'key_{i}', str(i))

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLiteCache.close
documentation: Close any open connections

An async interface for caching objects in a SQLite database.

Example:

> ```pycon
> >>> # Store data in two tables under the 'testdb' database
> >>> d1 = SQLiteCache('testdb', 'table1')
> >>> d2 = SQLiteCache('testdb', 'table2')
> ```

Args:

: filename: Database filename
  table_name: Table name
  use_temp: Store database in a temp directory (e.g., `/tmp/http_cache.sqlite`).

  > Note: if `cache_name` is an absolute path, this option will be ignored.

  kwargs: Additional keyword arguments for {py:func}`sqlite3.connect`

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.sqlite.SQLitePickleCache
documentation: Same as {py:class}`SqliteCache`, but pickles values before saving

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.__init__
documentation:
Args:

: cache_name: Cache prefix or namespace, depending on backend
  expire_after: Time after which a cache entry will be expired; see

  > {ref}`user_guide:cache expiration` for possible formats

  urls_expire_after: Expiration times to apply for different URL patterns
  allowed_codes: Only cache responses with these status codes
  allowed_methods: Only cache requests with these HTTP methods
  include_headers: Cache requests with different headers separately
  ignored_params: Request parameters to be excluded from the cache key
  autoclose: Close any active backend connections when the session is closed
  cache_control: Use Cache-Control response headers
  filter_fn: function that takes a {py:class}`aiohttp.ClientResponse` object and

  > returns a boolean indicating whether or not that response should be cached. Will be
  > applied to both new and previously cached responses

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.is_cacheable
documentation: Perform all checks needed to determine if the given response should be cached

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.create_cache_actions
documentation:
Create cache actions based on request info

Args:

: key: key from create_key function
  url: Request URL
  expire_after: Expiration time to set only for this request; overrides

  > `CachedSession.expire_after`, and accepts all the same values.

  refresh: Revalidate with the server before using a cached response, and refresh if needed

  : (e.g., a "soft refresh", like F5 in a browser)

  kwargs: All other request arguments

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.request
documentation:
Fetch a cached response based on cache actions

Args:
    actions: CacheActions from create_cache_actions function

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.get_response
documentation: Fetch a cached response based on a cache key

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend._get_redirect_response
documentation: Get the response referenced by a redirect key, if available

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.save_response
documentation:
Save a response to the cache

Args:
    response: Response to save
    cache_key: Cache key to use for the response; will be generated if not provided
    expires: Expiration time to set for the response

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.clear
documentation: Clear cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.delete
documentation: Delete a response from the cache, along with its history (if applicable)

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.delete_expired_responses
documentation:
Deletes all expired responses from the cache.
Also deletes any cache items that are filtered out according to `filter_fn()`.

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.create_key
documentation: Create a unique cache key based on request details

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.delete_url
documentation: Delete cached response associated with `url`, along with its history (if applicable)

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.has_url
documentation: Returns `True` if cache has `url`, `False` otherwise

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.get_urls
documentation: Get all URLs currently in the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend.close
documentation: Close any active connections, if applicable

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.CacheBackend._close_if_enabled
documentation: Close any active connections, if `autoclose` is enabled

Base class for cache backends; includes a non-persistent, in-memory cache.

This manages higher-level cache operations, including cache expiration, generating cache keys,
and managing redirect history.

Lower-level storage operations are handled by {py:class}`.BaseCache`.
To extend this with your own custom backend, implement one or more subclasses of
{py:class}`.BaseCache` to use as {py:attr}`CacheBackend.responses` and
{py:attr}`CacheBackend.response_aliases`.

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.serialize
documentation: Serialize a URL or response into bytes

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize a cached URL or response

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache._get_serializer
documentation:
Get the appropriate serializer to use; either `itsdangerous`, if a secret key is
specified, or plain `pickle` otherwise.
Raises:

> py:exc:`ImportError` if `secret_key` is specified but `itsdangerous` is not installed

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.contains
documentation: Check if a key is stored in the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.clear
documentation: Delete all items from the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.close
documentation: Close any active connections, if applicable

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.delete
documentation: Delete an item from the cache. Does not raise an error if the item is missing.

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.bulk_delete
documentation: Delete item(s) from the cache. Does not raise an error if the item is missing.

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.keys
documentation: Get all keys stored in the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.read
documentation: Read an item from the cache. Returns `None` if the item is missing.

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.size
documentation: Get the number of items in the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.values
documentation: Get all values stored in the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.write
documentation: Write an item to the cache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.BaseCache.pop
documentation: Delete an item from the cache, and return the deleted item

A wrapper for lower-level cache storage operations. This is separate from
{py:class}`.CacheBackend` to allow a single backend to contain multiple cache objects.

Args:

: secret_key: Optional secret key used to sign cache items for added security
  salt: Optional salt used to sign cache items
  serializer: Custom serializer that provides `loads` and `dumps` methods

classes.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.DictCache

methods.name: aiohttp_client_cache.backends.base.DictCache.read
documentation:
An additional step is needed here for response data. The original response object
is still in memory, and hasn't gone through a serialize/deserialize loop. So, the file-like
response body has already been read, and needs to be reset.
Simple in-memory storage that wraps a dict with the {py:class}`.BaseStorage` interface
